target.writeBoundary: Modify only index.js and test/index.js; do not change package metadata, dependencies, README API wording, escaping rules, or upstream repository state

userConfirmedIntent.summary: Use a real external behavior-change dogfood to prove PBE can recover existing stringification intent, implement a narrow Symbol input behavior change, and validate it with the project's own tests.
includedBehavior: Preserve existing escaping for quote, apostrophe, ampersand, less-than, and greater-than characters.
Preserve existing stringification behavior for undefined, null, numbers, and objects.
Change input coercion so Symbol values return their String(value) representation instead of throwing.
Add a focused test proving Symbol input returns Symbol(description).
Run npm test successfully after the change.
excludedBehavior: Do not change the escaped entity vocabulary.
Do not change the exported API name or module format.
Do not change package.json, dependencies, lint/test tooling, README API wording, benchmark files, or license/history files.
Do not create an upstream PR or claim maintainer approval.

observedAnchors.anchor: Escape special characters in the given string of text
observedAnchors.meaning: The public purpose is HTML text escaping, not arbitrary behavior expansion.

observedAnchors.anchor: This function will escape the following characters: `"`, `'`, `&`, `<`, and `>`.
observedAnchors.meaning: The escape vocabulary is explicit and must not change in this dogfood.
Input coercion happens before escape scanning and is the narrow implementation point for Symbol behavior.
The test suite already treats non-string inputs as stringified values.
The test suite protects the existing escape entity behavior.

candidateMatch.recommendedShape: Replace concatenation-based coercion with explicit String(value) and add one Symbol input test.
candidateMatch.whyThisShape: String(value) preserves the current outputs for existing tested inputs while avoiding the Symbol TypeError caused by string concatenation.
risksIfWrong: Changing the escape regex or entity strings would expand beyond the selected behavior slice.
Changing package metadata or dependencies would create tooling drift unrelated to Symbol behavior.
Treating observed README/tests as maintainer approval would violate retrofit boundaries.

implementationPlan.expectedFlow: Change input coercion in escapeHtml, add a Symbol test near existing non-string stringification tests, then run npm test.
nonGoals: No upstream branch, issue, or pull request creation.

forbiddenFlows.reason: The selected slice only changes pre-escape input coercion for Symbol values.
The project baseline tests already pass; tooling updates would obscure the behavior dogfood.
This is local PBE dogfood and does not claim maintainer acceptance.

build.summary: npm install completed in the local checkout before implementation, and npm test passed after implementation.

runtime.summary: The project's own Mocha test suite passed with 31 tests, including the new Symbol stringification case.

hardware.summary: escape-html has no hardware validation surface.

remainingRisks: This is a local behavior dogfood, not an upstream proposal.
Future API documentation changes would require a separate graph-backed slice.
nextAllowedActions: Review the local index.js and test/index.js diff.
Inspect the graph delta before any graph-source update.
Do not modify package metadata, dependencies, README wording, benchmarks, or upstream state in this dogfood.
